Introduced HTML_DIR=/usr/local/www/darkhttpd; replaced all $SCRIPT_DIR/work references in run.sh with $HTML_DIR; updated RESULTS_DIR default, generate_html_index, generate_html_skipped_report, show_last_test, clean messaging
Updated darkhttpd_dir from /usr/home/osvaldo/freebsd-ci-actions/work to /usr/local/www/darkhttpd (system default docroot)
Appended DECISION #000010: migrate HTML docroot from work/ to /usr/local/www/darkhttpd

Documented BUG pmc-002: repeated_process_cycles_have_bounded_skew flakiness — root cause independent pmc_start timing + idle workload + no CPU affinity; collector default 100 permille vs shell test default 250 permille

Generated v1 vs v2 comparison report: 475 v1 samples (0% fail, mean 42‰) vs 1404 v2 samples (95.3% fail — 94.7% low_count_warning); valid v2 subset mean 24.4‰ max 69.2‰; identified bug pmc-003 (timeout kills dd early)
Documented bug pmc-003: v2 timeout 5 terminates dd via SIGTERM before accumulating sufficient cycles; fix is dd count=N instead of timeout
Created v1/v2/v3 PMC skew investigation summary: sign-bias discovery (b>a 94-100%), H1/H2/H3 test design, pmc-001/pmc-002/pmc-003 bugs, recommendations
v3 collector run: 310 samples. H1 delta_abs=199K constant across 1-30s (fixed overhead). H2 b>a=94%. H3 dd=0.42‰ vs sleep=47‰ (113x reduction, 120x more cycles). Fixed overhead confirmed.
Rewrote v1/v2/v3 comparison MD with actual v3 empirical results. Key finding: fixed ~199K cycle offset from sequential pmc_start()/pmc_read(). dd count=500000 reduces skew 113x.
Created ATF v4 test: T1 calibrated_skew_is_near_zero (hard assert corrected<1permille), T2 arming_offset_is_stable (informational). Calibration via 'true' workload; measurement via dd count=500000.
v4 run: baseline_offset=197250 cycles. corrected_permille median=0.000000permille max=0.0125permille. PASS. Counters identical after calibration. Noise floor 0.21permille.
Created full v1/v2/v3/v4 comparison MD. Key: fixed 197K offset fully explained; corrected median=0.000permille; safe tolerance 0.1permille achievable with calibration.
Created tsc_utils.h: CPUID helpers (leaves 0x1/0x15/0x16/0x80000007), tsc_read() LFENCE+RDTSC, tsc_compute_frequency() 0x15→0x16 fallback, vendor/feature detection inlines. SWLSVROS-6589.

Created tsc_detect_test.c: 7 ATF cases TC-TSC-DET-01..07 — TSC feature bit, InvariantTSC, RDTSCP, ART ratio leaf, frequency bounds, monotonicity (1000 pairs), wall-clock cross-validation (2% threshold). SWLSVROS-6589.
Created tests/sys/amd/tsc/Makefile: out-of-tree ATF build, port ATF paths, tsc_detect_test as single ATF_TESTS_C target. SWLSVROS-6589.
Created tests/sys/amd/tsc/Kyuafile: single tsc_detect_test entry. SWLSVROS-6589.
Updated run.sh: added TSC suite to suite_src_dir, suite_install_dir, expand_suite_list (ALL), and get_test_meta (tsc_detect_test → TC-TSC-DET:CRITICAL). SWLSVROS-6589.

SWLSVROS-6556: tsc_drift_test.c created — 5 ATF cases (TC-TSC-DRF-01..05): same-CPU zero-delta, two-CPU atomic simultaneous read, 1000-iter oneshot, 50ms sleep freq agreement, AMD invariant soft-CHECK
SWLSVROS-6556: tsc_invariant_test.c created — 4 ATF cases (TC-TSC-INV-01..04): CPUID gate, 5x10ms rate windows <1%, 500 sched_yield strict monotone, 200ms busy loop <0.5% vs CPUID
SWLSVROS-6556: tsc_stress_test.c created — 3 ATF cases (TC-TSCSTR-01..03): 120s monotone poll under xorshift64+32MiB-stride stressors, 12x10s freq <2%, 10M rapid rdtsc all non-decreasing
